questions.question: Which transport protocol and port does SSH use?
questions.explanation: SSH uses TCP port 22. Telnet uses TCP port 23.

questions.question: Why is SSH preferred over Telnet for remote CLI access?
options: SSH encrypts the session; Telnet sends data in plain text
SSH supports multicast but Telnet does not
questions.explanation: SSH encrypts all session data including passwords, while Telnet transmits everything in plain text — making SSH the secure choice.

questions.question: Which SSH version should be used on modern Cisco devices?
questions.explanation: SSHv2 (released 2006) is more secure and preferred. SSHv1 is legacy. v1.99 is not a real version — it just means the device supports both v1 and v2.

questions.question: Which two prerequisites are required before 'crypto key generate rsa' will succeed?
options: An access list and a loopback interface
A non-default hostname and a configured domain name
Telnet enabled and VTY passwords set
NTP synchronization and a console password
questions.explanation: RSA keys are named using the FQDN, so a non-default hostname and a 'ip domain name' configuration are required before the key will generate.

questions.question: Which command restricts the VTY lines to allow only SSH?
questions.explanation: 'transport input ssh' on the VTY lines permits only SSH connections; Telnet is denied.

questions.question: What is SSHv2's minimum required RSA key modulus length?
questions.explanation: SSHv2 requires at least a 768-bit RSA key. Common choices are 1024 or 2048 bits for stronger security.

questions.question: What IOS image naming indicator shows that cryptographic features like SSH are supported?
questions.explanation: 'K9' in the IOS image name indicates cryptographic support (SSH, IPsec, etc.). NPE (No Payload Encryption) images, used in restricted countries, do NOT support SSH.

questions.question: Which command applies an ACL to the VTY lines to restrict who may connect via SSH/Telnet?
questions.explanation: 'access-class <acl> in' applies an ACL to VTY lines — different from 'ip access-group' which applies to interfaces.

questions.question: On a Layer 2 switch, which interface provides the management IP used for remote access?
options: The first physical port in VLAN 1
questions.explanation: A Layer 2 switch uses an SVI (interface vlan <id>) to host its management IP for remote access.

questions.question: Why is 'ip default-gateway' used on a Layer 2 switch?
options: To let the switch reach remote networks — it has no routing table
To act as the DHCP default gateway for clients
questions.explanation: Layer 2 switches don't route, so 'ip default-gateway' tells the switch where to send traffic destined outside its local subnet (for management purposes).

questions.question: Which command creates a local username with an encrypted password?
questions.explanation: 'username <name> secret <pw>' stores the password as a hash. Pair it with 'login local' on the line to authenticate against the local user DB.

questions.question: How many VTY lines are typically available on a Cisco device and what's the common configuration range?
questions.explanation: Modern Cisco devices support up to 16 VTY lines numbered 0-15, commonly configured with 'line vty 0 15'.
